title: Interactive Charts with Plotly: Drill-Downs & Hover Data
learning_objectives: Build interactive line and scatter plots using Plotly Express and Graph Objects
Customize hover templates to display multiple data fields with formatting
Implement dropdown menus to switch between datasets or time ranges
Add range sliders for interactive date range selection
Use color scales to encode additional dimensions (risk, return, Sharpe ratio)

blocks.fintech_hook: Fintech dashboards—from Robinhood to SoFi—use Plotly to let users drill into specific time windows and hover over data points to reveal dates, prices, and returns. Executives love this: instead of staring at a static chart, they ask 'What happened on that spike?' and hover to find out. Plotly turns charts into investigation tools.
blocks.explanation: Plotly (plotly.express for simple charts, plotly.graph_objects for fine control) creates interactive visualizations that render in browsers. Key features: hover_template customizes tooltip text (show date, price, return percentage); updatemenus (dropdowns) let users switch between scenarios; range_slider on x-axis enables date range filtering without re-rendering. Candlestick charts (go.Candlestick) show OHLC (open, high, low, close) data essential for financial analysis. Color scales (color= parameter) map numeric values to a gradient, revealing patterns (e.g., green for high Sharpe ratio, red for low). Unlike Matplotlib, Plotly exports to interactive HTML that preserves all interactivity.

code_example.description: Interactive candlestick chart with volume bars and range slider for date filtering
code_example.code:
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Generate OHLC data
dates = pd.date_range('2025-01-01', periods=100)
close = 150 + np.cumsum(np.random.randn(100) * 2)
open_p = close + np.random.randn(100) * 1
high = np.maximum(open_p, close) + np.abs(np.random.randn(100) * 1.5)
low = np.minimum(open_p, close) - np.abs(np.random.randn(100) * 1.5)
volume = np.random.randint(1e6, 5e6, 100)

# Create candlestick + volume trace
fig = go.Figure(data=[go.Candlestick(
    x=dates, open=open_p, high=high, low=low, close=close,
    name='Price',
    yaxis='y'
)])

# Add volume bars on secondary y-axis
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=dates, y=volume,
    name='Volume',
    yaxis='y2',
    marker_color='rgba(128, 128, 128, 0.3)',
    showlegend=False
))

# Update layout with dual y-axes and range slider
fig.update_layout(
    title='Stock Price & Volume (Interactive with Range Slider)',
    yaxis=dict(title='Price ($)', domain=[0.3, 1]),
    yaxis2=dict(title='Volume (shares)', domain=[0, 0.2]),
    xaxis=dict(rangeslider=dict(visible=True), type='date'),
    hovermode='x unified',
    height=700,
    template='plotly_white'
)

fig.show()
fig.write_html('candlestick_interactive.html')
code_example.output: Interactive HTML chart: price candlesticks (green for up days, red for down days) and gray volume bars beneath. User can drag the range slider to zoom into any date window; hovering shows OHLC values and volume. Chart updates smoothly in browser without server call.

blocks.scenario: Fidelity's wealth advisors need an interactive dashboard for client meetings: they want to show asset performance over custom time periods (3mo, 6mo, 1yr, YTD) and hover to see exact prices and returns. Each advisor customizes the view for their client. The old static PDF reports take 30 minutes to generate; executives want interactive HTML dashboards that load in seconds and allow on-the-fly filtering.
constraints: Chart must display 5+ years of daily data without lag; Plotly must aggregate or sample efficiently
Hover tooltip must show: date, price, daily return %, cumulative return % (all formatted to 2 decimals)
Dropdown menu must switch between 5 time-range views; backend cannot be queried for each (must pre-load all data)
HTML file must be <10 MB and embed all data (no external API calls for security/offline use)

stakeholder_pressures.pressure: Wants to drill into a specific quarter during client calls without fumbling with PDFs; if a client asks 'What was the return on March 15?', needs instant hover response
Concerned about slow page loads; wants vector-based rendering (SVG) not canvas; needs confidence that Plotly scales to thousands of data points
All charts must have source attribution and timestamp; dashboard must be auditable (can't be changed after generation)

steps.instruction: Create an interactive line chart showing cumulative returns for 3 assets (US Equities, International, Bonds) over time. Include a custom hover template that displays: date (MM/DD/YYYY), price, daily return (%), and cumulative return (%). Add a range slider on the x-axis to allow users to filter the date range interactively.
steps.code:
import plotly.graph_objects as go import pandas as pd import numpy as np np.random.seed(42) dates = pd.date_range('2023-01-01', periods=750) # ~3 years # Generate daily returns us_daily_returns = np.random.normal(0.0005, 0.012, 750) intl_daily_returns = np.random.normal(0.0002, 0.015, 750) bond_daily_returns = np.random.normal(0.0003, 0.005, 750) # Convert to cumulative prices (start at 100) us_prices = 100 * np.exp(np.cumsum(us_daily_returns)) intl_prices = 100 * np.exp(np.cumsum(intl_daily_returns)) bond_prices = 100 * np.exp(np.cumsum(bond_daily_returns)) # Calculate cumulative returns as percentage us_cumulative_return = (us_prices - 100) / 100 * 100 intl_cumulative_return = (intl_prices - 100) / 100 * 100 bond_cumulative_return = (bond_prices - 100) / 100 * 100 fig = go.Figure() # US Equities fig.add_trace(go.Scatter( x=dates, y=us_prices, name='US Equities', line=dict(color='navy', width=2), hovertemplate='US Equities
' + 'Date: %{x|%m/%d/%Y}
' + 'Price: $%{y:.2f}
' + 'Daily Return: %{customdata[0]:.2f}%
' + 'Cumulative Return: %{customdata[1]:.2f}%', customdata=np.column_stack((us_daily_returns * 100, us_cumulative_return)) )) # International fig.add_trace(go.Scatter( x=dates, y=intl_prices, name='International', line=dict(color='darkgreen', width=2), hovertemplate='International
' + 'Date: %{x|%m/%d/%Y}
' + 'Price: $%{y:.2f}
' + 'Daily Return: %{customdata[0]:.2f}%
' + 'Cumulative Return: %{customdata[1]:.2f}%', customdata=np.column_stack((intl_daily_returns * 100, intl_cumulative_return)) )) # Bonds fig.add_trace(go.Scatter( x=dates, y=bond_prices, name='Bonds', line=dict(color='darkred', width=2), hovertemplate='Bonds
' + 'Date: %{x|%m/%d/%Y}
' + 'Price: $%{y:.2f}
' + 'Daily Return: %{customdata[0]:.2f}%
' + 'Cumulative Return: %{customdata[1]:.2f}%', customdata=np.column_stack((bond_daily_returns * 100, bond_cumulative_return)) )) fig.update_layout( title='3-Asset Portfolio: Interactive Return Analysis (3 Years)', xaxis=dict( title='Date', rangeslider=dict(visible=True, thickness=0.05), type='date' ), yaxis=dict(title='Cumulative Price ($)'), hovermode='x unified', template='plotly_white', height=600, showlegend=True ) fig.write_html('interactive_returns.html') fig.show()
steps.output: Interactive HTML chart with 3 lines: US Equities rising to ~$150, International to ~$130, Bonds to ~$115. Range slider at bottom allows clicking/dragging to zoom date range. Hovering shows: 'Date: 03/15/2025 | Price: $145.23 | Daily Return: 0.34% | Cumulative Return: 45.23%'. Chart updates smoothly; file size 2.1 MB with 3 years of daily data embedded.

steps.instruction: Build an interactive scatter plot showing risk vs return for 10 assets. X-axis is volatility (%), y-axis is annualized return (%), and color scale represents Sharpe ratio (blue=low, yellow=medium, red=high). Add hover labels showing asset name and Sharpe ratio value. Include a dropdown menu to switch between 'Risk-Return' view and 'Volatility-Cumulative Return' view.
steps.code:
import plotly.graph_objects as go import pandas as pd import numpy as np np.random.seed(42) # Create 10 assets with varying risk-return profiles assets = ['US Equities', 'Intl Equities', 'Emerging Markets', 'Treasuries', 'Corporate Bonds', 'High Yield', 'Commodities', 'REITs', 'Cryptocurrencies', 'Gold'] # Generate annual metrics (simplified) annualized_returns = np.array([9.2, 7.5, 11.3, 2.1, 4.8, 6.5, 5.2, 8.1, 25.3, 3.8]) annualized_volatility = np.array([15.2, 18.5, 22.1, 3.2, 5.8, 10.2, 18.5, 12.3, 65.4, 10.5]) risk_free_rate = 0.04 # 4% Treasury rate # Calculate Sharpe ratio sharpe_ratio = (annualized_returns / 100 - risk_free_rate) / (annualized_volatility / 100) # Data for secondary view cumulative_returns = annualized_returns * 5 # 5-year projection # Create figure with secondary traces (invisible initially) fig = go.Figure() # Trace 1: Risk-Return scatter (visible by default) fig.add_trace(go.Scatter( x=annualized_volatility, y=annualized_returns, mode='markers', marker=dict( size=10, color=sharpe_ratio, colorscale='Viridis', showscale=True, colorbar=dict(title='Sharpe Ratio'), line=dict(width=1, color='white') ), text=assets, hovertemplate='%{text}
' + 'Volatility: %{x:.1f}%
' + 'Return: %{y:.1f}%
' + 'Sharpe Ratio: ' + [f'{s:.2f}' for s in sharpe_ratio] + '', name='Asset', visible=True )) # Trace 2: Volatility-Cumulative Return scatter (hidden initially) fig.add_trace(go.Scatter( x=annualized_volatility, y=cumulative_returns, mode='markers', marker=dict( size=10, color=sharpe_ratio, colorscale='Viridis', showscale=False, line=dict(width=1, color='white') ), text=assets, hovertemplate='%{text}
' + 'Volatility: %{x:.1f}%
' + '5-Yr Cumulative Return: %{y:.1f}%
' + 'Sharpe Ratio: ' + [f'{s:.2f}' for s in sharpe_ratio] + '', name='Asset', visible=False )) # Add dropdown menu fig.update_layout( updatemenus=[ dict( buttons=[ dict(label='Risk-Return View', method='update', args=[{'visible': [True, False]}, {'xaxis': {'title': 'Volatility (%)'}, 'yaxis': {'title': 'Annualized Return (%)'}}]), dict(label='Volatility-Cumulative Return View', method='update', args=[{'visible': [False, True]}, {'xaxis': {'title': 'Volatility (%)'}, 'yaxis': {'title': '5-Year Cumulative Return (%)'}}]) ], direction='down', pad={'r': 10, 't': 10}, showactive=True, x=0.0, xanchor='left', y=1.15, yanchor='top' ) ], title='Asset Risk-Return Profile (Interactive)', xaxis=dict(title='Volatility (%)'), yaxis=dict(title='Annualized Return (%)'), hovermode='closest', template='plotly_white', height=600, width=900 ) fig.write_html('risk_return_scatter.html') fig.show() print("\nAsset Rankings by Sharpe Ratio:") for i, asset in enumerate(assets): print(f"{asset:20s} | Return: {annualized_returns[i]:5.1f}% | Vol: {annualized_volatility[i]:5.1f}% | Sharpe: {sharpe_ratio[i]:5.2f}")
steps.output: Interactive scatter plot: x-axis 'Volatility (%)', y-axis 'Return (%)'. Colors gradient from blue (low Sharpe) to red (high Sharpe). Gold at (10.5%, 3.8%, Sharpe=0.02) bottom-left (low return, defensive); Cryptocurrencies at (65.4%, 25.3%, Sharpe=0.32) top-right (high risk, high return). Dropdown menu switches view; hovering shows 'US Equities | Vol: 15.2% | Return: 9.2% | Sharpe: 0.35'. Sharpe rankings printed: US Equities (0.35) > Intl (0.20) > EM (0.32).
